agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Subscription billing platform managing lifecycle states (activation, renewal, cancellation) with payment validation, retry logic, tiered grace periods, and free trial constraints.","bounded_context":"Subscription Billing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Subscription","definition":"A recurring billing agreement between a customer and the platform, with a defined billing cycle and tier."},{"term":"Payment Method","definition":"A stored instrument (card, bank account) used to collect subscription fees. Has a status including 'failed'."},{"term":"Billing Cycle","definition":"The recurring period (e.g., monthly, annual) that defines when payment is due."},{"term":"Renewal","definition":"The automated process of charging for the next billing cycle, initiated 7 days before the current cycle ends."},{"term":"Renewal Attempt","definition":"A single try to collect payment during the renewal window. Up to 3 attempts are made for standard tiers."},{"term":"Grace Period","definition":"A time window (14 days for Premium) during which a subscription remains active despite failed payments, replacing the 3-strike rule."},{"term":"Cancelled Subscription","definition":"A terminal state — the subscription is permanently ended and cannot be reactivated."},{"term":"Premium Tier","definition":"A subscription level that receives a 14-day grace period instead of the standard 3-strike cancellation rule."},{"term":"Free Trial","definition":"A zero-cost introductory subscription. Only one active trial is permitted per email address at any time."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A subscription cannot be activated with a failed payment method on file.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state integrity constraint. Activating a subscription against a known-bad payment method would create a subscription that can never collect revenue — the system enters a corrupt state where an 'active' subscription has no viable funding source. This is a precondition guard, not a tunable policy.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When activateSubscription() is called AND the associated payment method status is 'failed', THEN the operation is rejected and the subscription does NOT transition to 'active'."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Subscriptions auto-renew 7 days before the billing cycle ends to allow retry time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 7-day lead time is a business policy tunable. A VP could change this to 5 or 10 days based on payment processor retry windows or business strategy. The system is not corrupt if the window changes — only the retry opportunity shrinks or expands.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: The renewal process is triggered exactly 7 days before the billing cycle end date."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"After 3 failed renewal attempts, the subscription is automatically cancelled.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The retry count of 3 is a configurable business policy threshold. Management could decide to allow 5 retries or 2 retries based on cost-of-retry vs. churn analysis. Changing the number does not corrupt system state.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a standard-tier subscription accumulates 3 consecutive failed renewal attempts, the subscription transitions to 'cancelled' automatically."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A cancelled subscription cannot be reactivated — customers must create a new subscription.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state machine integrity constraint. 'Cancelled' is a terminal state. Allowing reactivation would violate the directed acyclic nature of the subscription lifecycle, creating ambiguity around billing history, trial eligibility, and audit trails. If a cancelled subscription could be reactivated, the system's historical records become unreliable.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When reactivateSubscription() is called on a subscription with status 'cancelled', THEN the operation is rejected and status remains 'cancelled'."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Premium tier subscribers get a 14-day grace period before cancellation instead of the 3-strike rule.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 14-day window and the decision to use time-based vs. attempt-based logic for Premium are business policy decisions. The grace period length is a tunable value driven by customer retention strategy and competitive positioning. Changing it to 21 days does not corrupt system state.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a Premium-tier subscription has a failed renewal, the subscription remains active for 14 days from the first failure. If payment is not successfully collected within that 14-day window, the subscription transitions to 'cancelled'."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Free trial subscriptions cannot have more than one active trial per email address at any time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a data consistency constraint that prevents revenue leakage and abuse. Allowing duplicate active trials per email would mean the uniqueness guarantee is broken — the system is in an inconsistent state where a single customer consumes multiple free resources simultaneously. This is analogous to a unique constraint in a database; violating it corrupts the business model.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When createTrialSubscription() is called for an email address that already has an active trial subscription, THEN the operation is rejected and no new trial is created."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer attempts to activate a subscription","when":"The payment method on file has a 'failed' status","then":"The activation is rejected with a clear error indicating the payment method must be updated"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An active subscription with a valid billing cycle end date","when":"The current date is exactly 7 days before the billing cycle end date","then":"The system initiates the auto-renewal payment process"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A standard-tier subscription in renewal with 2 prior failed attempts","when":"The 3rd renewal attempt also fails","then":"The subscription is automatically transitioned to 'cancelled' status"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A subscription in 'cancelled' status","when":"Any attempt is made to reactivate or resume the subscription","then":"The operation is rejected and the customer is directed to create a new subscription"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A Premium-tier subscription with a failed renewal attempt","when":"14 days elapse from the first failed attempt without a successful payment","then":"The subscription is automatically transitioned to 'cancelled' status"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A Premium-tier subscription with a failed renewal attempt","when":"Payment succeeds within the 14-day grace window","then":"The subscription remains active and the grace period is cleared"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A customer with an active free trial subscription","when":"The same email address attempts to create another free trial","then":"The second trial creation is rejected with an error indicating an active trial already exists"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Payment method fails between subscription creation and activation","expected_behavior":"The activation check must evaluate payment method status at the moment of activation, not at creation time. A method valid at creation but failed at activation must block activation."},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber's payment method is fixed on day 14 exactly","expected_behavior":"Business must define whether day 14 is inclusive or exclusive. Recommendation: inclusive (payment on day 14 still saves the subscription)."},{"scenario":"3rd renewal attempt fails at the exact same moment as the billing cycle ends","expected_behavior":"Cancellation takes precedence. The subscription is cancelled regardless of cycle boundary timing."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels and immediately tries to create a new subscription with the same payment method that previously failed","expected_behavior":"BR-1 still applies: the new subscription cannot be activated until the payment method is resolved, even though creating a new subscription is allowed."},{"scenario":"Free trial expires naturally and customer wants another trial later","expected_behavior":"BR-6 says 'at any time' — once the first trial is no longer active (expired/completed), a new trial should be permitted. The constraint is on concurrent active trials, not lifetime trial count."},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber downgrades to standard tier while in a grace period","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — does the 14-day grace period persist or immediately switch to the 3-strike rule? Needs product clarification."},{"scenario":"Multiple email aliases pointing to the same mailbox attempt separate trials","expected_behavior":"The uniqueness constraint is on the email address string, not the underlying mailbox. user+1@example.com and user+2@example.com would be treated as different addresses unless normalization rules are defined."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Failed payment handling directly impacts revenue recovery. The 7-day retry window and 3-strike/14-day grace policies define the trade-off between churn and bad debt. Getting these wrong either loses paying customers prematurely or extends free service to non-paying accounts.","operational":"Auto-renewal timing and retry logic must integrate with payment processor APIs and retry scheduling. The Premium grace period introduces a separate code path that must be maintained and monitored independently.","user":"Customers expect clear communication at each stage — failed payment notifications, grace period warnings, and cancellation finality. The 'no reactivation' rule (BR-4) has the highest friction; UX must clearly guide users to create a new subscription.","risk":"Trial abuse (BR-6 violation) is the primary fraud vector. Email aliasing and disposable email services can circumvent naive uniqueness checks. The terminal cancellation rule (BR-4) carries legal/regulatory risk if customers dispute the inability to restore service.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (Standard Tier)","impact":"Subject to 3-strike cancellation; need clear retry failure notifications to update payment before losing access."},{"group":"Customers (Premium Tier)","impact":"14-day grace period provides more cushion but must be clearly communicated so they don't assume unlimited grace."},{"group":"Finance/Revenue Team","impact":"Retry windows and grace periods directly affect MRR recognition timing and bad debt write-off schedules."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will handle escalations from cancelled subscribers who want reactivation — must be trained on the terminal state policy and new-subscription flow."},{"group":"Fraud/Abuse Team","impact":"Trial uniqueness constraint is their primary lever; must define email normalization strategy."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is well-articulated with clear rules and explicit thresholds. Confidence is below 80 because several edge cases remain unaddressed: Premium grace period boundary conditions, email normalization for trial uniqueness, and the interaction between tier changes and active grace periods. No contradictions detected.","high_confidence_areas":["Terminal cancellation state is clearly defined as invariant","Payment method validation at activation is a clear precondition","Trial uniqueness per email is an explicit constraint","Standard-tier 3-strike rule is unambiguous"],"low_confidence_areas":["Premium grace period boundary: is day 14 inclusive or exclusive?","Interaction between tier downgrade and active grace period","Email normalization rules for trial uniqueness (aliases, plus-addressing, case sensitivity)","Whether '3 failed attempts' means 3 consecutive or 3 total within a renewal window"],"assumptions":["The 3 failed attempts for standard tier are consecutive within a single renewal cycle, not cumulative across cycles","The 14-day grace period for Premium replaces the 3-strike rule entirely (not in addition to it)","'Active trial' means a trial in 'active' status, not merely 'created' — expired or completed trials do not block new ones","Payment method 'failed' status is a system-known state, not inferred at charge time"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
